measures.name: WEIGHT
measures.description: This measure represents the maximum net product weight across all products.

measures.name: PALMEASURE
measures.description: This measure calculates the maximum number of cartons per pallet across all products.

measures.name: FORECASTPRODUCTION
measures.description: Calculates the total quantity from the PLAN table, filtering for a specific main account ID and model ID, while ignoring any filters on the DEPARTMENT and P&L_ACCOUNTS tables.

measures.name: ACTUAL $
measures.description: Calculates the sum of the 'VALUE' column from the 'GL' table, but only for dates up to the maximum date in the 'PERIODENDDATES' column of the 'PERIODENDDATES' table.

measures.name: ACTUAL $ PY
measures.description: This measure calculates the estimated value from the previous fiscal year.

measures.name: ESTIMATE
measures.description: This measure calculates the sum of the actual dollar amount up to the maximum date in the period end dates, and the forecasted dollar amount for dates beyond the maximum date in the model with a specific model ID.

measures.name: FORECAST $
measures.description: This measure calculates the sum of the 'Value' field in the 'PLAN' table for the model ID that matches a specific string literal, but only for fiscal years 2023 and onwards.

measures.name: ESTIMATE2
measures.description: This measure calculates the value of ESTIMATE and multiplies it by -1, effectively reversing the sign of the ESTIMATE.

measures.name: FORECAST_ABSOLUTE $
measures.description: This measure calculates the negative value of the 'FORECAST $' measure for fiscal years starting from 2023.

measures.name: ESTIMATEGSV
measures.description: This measure calculates the sum of the actual gross sales value up to a maximum date, and then adds the sum of the planned gross sales value for dates beyond the maximum date, with specific filters applied to P&L accounts, department, and model ID.

measures.name: FORECASTGSV
measures.description: Calculates the sum of the Gross Sales Value (GSV) from the PLAN table, filtered by specific MAINACCOUNTID and MODELID.

measures.name: ACTUALGSV
measures.description: Calculates the total value from the 'GL' table, filtered by a specific group in the 'P&L_ACCOUNTS' table and a specific model ID in the 'MODELID' table.

measures.name: ESTIMATE PROD-KG
measures.description: This measure calculates the sum of actual kilograms up to a maximum date, and adds the forecasted kilograms for dates beyond the maximum date, considering specific model and group conditions, while ignoring any filters on the Category, P&L Accounts, and Department fields.

measures.name: FORECAST $/CARTON
measures.description: Calculates the forecasted dollar amount per carton by dividing the 'FORECAST $ 2' measure by the 'FORECASTPRODUCTION' measure, considering all departments, cost types, accounts, account categories, and account level 2 values.

measures.name: FORECASTNSV
measures.description: Calculates the forecasted dollar amount for specific groups in the 'P&L_ACCOUNTS' table.

measures.name: FORECASTKG
measures.description: Calculates the total of the 'KG' column from the 'PLAN' table, filtering for a specific 'MAINACCOUNTID' in the 'P&L_ACCOUNTS' table and a specific 'MODELID', while ignoring any filters on the 'DEPARTMENT', 'ACCOUNT', 'ACCOUNTCATEGORY', 'ACCOUNT LEVEL 2', and 'COSTTYPE' columns of the 'P&L_ACCOUNTS' table.

measures.name: ACTUALINVOICEDPALLETS
measures.description: This measure calculates the total number of pallets from sales invoices up to the maximum period end date, ignoring any filters on the department and P&L accounts.

measures.name: FORECASTPALLETS
measures.description: Calculates the total number of pallets in the plan, filtering for a specific main account ID and model ID, while ignoring any filters on the department, account, account category, account level 2, and cost type.

measures.name: ACTUAL $ 2
measures.description: This measure calculates the negative value of the 'ACTUAL $' measure.

measures.name: ACTUALINVOICEDUNITS
measures.description: Calculates the total number of cartons in sales invoices.

measures.name: ESTIMATEPALLETS
measures.description: Calculates the sum of invoiced cartons divided by pallet measure for all products up to the maximum period end date, and adds the forecasted pallets for dates beyond the maximum period end date, considering all departments, P&L accounts, and categories, and where the model ID equals a specific string literal.

measures.name: ACTUALPRODUCTIONKG
measures.description: Calculates the sum of the 'ACTUALKG' column from the 'ProdTable' table, but only for rows where the 'CATEGORYGROUP' column in the 'PRODUCTS' table equals a specific string, the 'PRODSTATUS' column in the 'ProdTable' table equals one of three specific strings, and the 'DATES' column in the 'DATEV3' table is less than or equal to the maximum date in the 'PERIODENDDATES' column of the 'PERIODENDDATES' table.

measures.name: ACTUALNSV $
measures.description: Calculates the actual dollar value for specific groups in the 'P&L_ACCOUNTS' table.

measures.name: DYNAMIC ACTUAL
measures.description: This measure, DYNAMIC ACTUAL, conditionally displays the absolute value of the ACTUAL $ measure if the 'P&L_ACCOUNTS' table's GROUP column is filtered, otherwise it displays the ACTUAL $ measure as is.

measures.name: BLANK
measures.description: A measure that returns a blank value.

measures.name: DYNAMIC FORECAST
measures.description: This measure, DYNAMIC FORECAST, conditionally returns the absolute value of the FORECAST $ measure if the GROUP column in the P&L_ACCOUNTS table is filtered, otherwise it returns the original FORECAST $ measure.

measures.name: ESTIMATE GSV PY
measures.description: Calculates the estimated Gross Sales Value (GSV) for the previous fiscal year.

measures.name: ACTUALKG PY
measures.description: This measure calculates the actual kilograms for the previous fiscal year.

measures.name: PVM-VOLUME
measures.description: Calculates the difference between the actual kilograms year to date and the forecasted kilograms year to date, then multiplies this difference by the forecasted dollar per kilogram year to date.

measures.name: PVM-PRICE
measures.description: This measure calculates the difference between the estimated price per kilogram and the year-to-date forecasted price per kilogram, multiplied by the actual kilograms, for all dates up to the maximum period end date.

measures.name: PVM NET
measures.description: Calculates the net value by adding the PVM (Price Volume Mix) price and the PVM volume.

measures.name: BUDGET $
measures.description: Calculates the total budget value from the PLAN table for the model with a specific ID, considering only fiscal years from 2023 onwards.

measures.name: BUDGETKG
measures.description: Calculates the total of the 'KG' column from the 'PLAN' table, filtering for a specific 'MAINACCOUNTID' in the 'P&L_ACCOUNTS' table and a specific 'MODELID', while ignoring filters on 'DEPARTMENT', 'ACCOUNT', 'ACCOUNTCATEGORY', 'ACCOUNT LEVEL 2', and 'COSTTYPE' in the 'P&L_ACCOUNTS' table.

measures.name: FORECAST $ NY
measures.description: This measure calculates the estimated value for the next fiscal year based on the currently selected fiscal year in the 'DATEV3' table.

measures.name: FORECASTGSV $ NY
measures.description: This measure calculates the estimated value for the next fiscal year, specifically for the account identified by a certain string literal.

measures.name: BUDGETGSV
measures.description: Calculates the total Gross Sales Value (GSV) from the PLAN table, filtered by specific MAINACCOUNTID in the P&L_ACCOUNTS table and MODELID in the MODELID table.

measures.name: BUDGETPALLETS
measures.description: Calculates the total sum of pallets in the plan, considering only specific main account ID and model ID, while ignoring the filters on department, account, account category, account level 2, and cost type.

measures.name: MEASURECOUNT
measures.description: Counts the unique names of measures in the model details.

measures.name: BUDGET %
measures.description: The `BUDGET %` measure calculates the budget percentage by dividing the `[BUDGET $]` by the total budgeted gross sales value (`[BUDGETGSV]`). The `CALCULATE` function removes any filters from the `.P&L_ACCOUNTS` and `.DEPARTMENT` tables using the `ALL` function, ensuring that the percentage is based on the overall budgeted GSV without any specific filters.

measures.name: FORECAST %
measures.description: The `FORECAST %` measure calculates the forecast percentage by dividing the forecasted value (`[FORECAST $]`) by the total forecasted gross sales value (`[FORECASTGSV]`). The `CALCULATE` function removes any filters from the `.P&L_ACCOUNTS` and `.DEPARTMENT` tables using the `ALL` function, ensuring the percentage is based on the overall forecasted GSV without any specific filters applied.

measures.name: PLANVALUE
measures.description:
The `PLANVALUE` measure calculates the total value from the `PLAN[Value]` column by combining two components: promotional and other account values, filtered by the fiscal year starting from 2023. Here's how it works:

1. **Promo Calculation (`promo`)**:
   - **CALCULATE(SUM(PLAN[Value])...)**: Sums up the values in the `PLAN[Value]` column.
   - **Filter by `MAINACCOUNTID` = "63600"**: Applies a filter to include only records where `MAINACCOUNTID` equals "63600", which typically represents promotional accounts.
   - **USERELATIONSHIP(PLAN[DATE], '.DATE'[DATES])**: Uses an alternative relationship between the `PLAN[DATE]` and `.DATE'[DATES]` columns for this calculation.
   - **Filter by Fiscal Year**: Further filters the data to include only records where the fiscal year (`.DATE'[FISCALYEAR]`) is 2023 or later.

2. **Other Calculation (`other`)**:
   - **CALCULATE(SUM(PLAN[Value])...)**: Sums up the values in the `PLAN[Value]` column.
   - **Filter by `MAINACCOUNTID` <> "63600"**: Applies a filter to include only records where `MAINACCOUNTID` is not "63600", capturing all other account values.
   - **Filter by Fiscal Year**: Similar to the promo calculation, this filter ensures that only records from fiscal year 2023 or later are included.

3. **Return**:
   - **promo + other**: Adds the two calculated components together, giving the total plan value, including both promotional and other account values, for fiscal year 2023 and beyond.

This measure allows you to separate and sum promotional and non-promotional values for a specific fiscal period, providing a comprehensive total plan value.

measures.name: PLAN %
measures.description:
The `PLAN %` measure calculates the percentage of `PLANVALUE` relative to the total Gross Sales Value (GSV) for a specific account. Here's how it works:

1. **[PLANVALUE]**: This represents the value you're measuring against the total GSV.

2. **CALCULATE(SUM(PLAN[GSV]), '.P&L_ACCOUNTS'[MAINACCOUNTID] = "40100", ALL('.P&L_ACCOUNTS'))**: This part calculates the total GSV by summing the `PLAN[GSV]` column. It filters the data to include only records where `MAINACCOUNTID` is "40100" (which typically represents revenue). The `ALL('.P&L_ACCOUNTS')` function removes any filters on the `'.P&L_ACCOUNTS'` table to ensure the calculation considers the entire dataset for that account.

3. **DIVIDE(... , ...)**: The `DIVIDE` function then divides `[PLANVALUE]` by the calculated GSV to express the plan value as a percentage of total GSV. If the denominator (total GSV) is zero, the function returns a blank to avoid division by zero errors.

This measure gives you the percentage of `PLANVALUE` in relation to the total revenue (GSV) for the specific account "40100".

measures.name: FIXEDCOSTS
measures.description: The `FIXEDCOSTS` measure calculates the total fixed costs by summing the `PLAN[Value]` column for entries where the `COSTGROUP` in the `.P&L_ACCOUNTS` table is "FIXED". The `ABS` function is then applied to return the absolute value of this sum, ensuring that the result is always a positive number, regardless of whether the costs are recorded as negative or positive. This measure provides the total fixed costs, essential for understanding the fixed portion of the cost structure.

measures.name: PLANGSV
measures.description:
The `PLANGSV` measure calculates the total Gross Sales Value (GSV) from the `PLAN[GSV]` column, but only for records where the `MAINACCOUNTID` in the `.P&L_ACCOUNTS` table is "40100". Here's how it works:

1. **SUM(PLAN[GSV])**: Sums up the values in the `PLAN[GSV]` column, which represents the Gross Sales Value.

2. **CALCULATE(..., '.P&L_ACCOUNTS'[MAINACCOUNTID] = "40100")**: The `CALCULATE` function applies a filter that includes only those records where `MAINACCOUNTID` equals "40100" (typically representing revenue).

This measure provides the total Gross Sales Value specifically for the revenue account defined by `MAINACCOUNTID = "40100"`.

measures.name: GLASOFDATE
measures.description: The `GLASOFDATE` measure calculates the most recent date and time from the `GeneralJournalAccountEntry[LastProcessedChange_DateTime]` column, adds 10 hours to it, and then formats this adjusted date and time as "dd/MM/yyyy hh:mm:ss AM/PM". This adjusted date represents the "as of" date for general ledger entries, accounting for a specific time offset.

measures.name: CARTONS_UOM
measures.description: The `CARTONS_UOM` measure returns the maximum value from the `Ctn` column in the `.PRODUCTS` table. This value represents the largest carton unit of measure for the products listed in that table.

measures.name: KG_UOM
measures.description: The `KG_UOM` measure returns the maximum value from the `NETPRODUCTWEIGHT` column in the `.PRODUCTS` table. This value represents the heaviest net product weight in kilograms for the products listed in that table.

measures.name: ACTUALPRODUCTIONQTY
measures.description:
The `ACTUALPRODUCTIONQTY` measure calculates the total quantity of good products produced, filtered by specific conditions:

1. **SUM(ProdTableJour[QtyGood])**: Sums up the quantity of good products (`QtyGood`) from the `ProdTableJour` table.

2. **CALCULATE(..., '.PRODUCTS'[CATEGORYGROUP] = "Brand", ProdTable[ProdStatus_$Label] = "Completed" || ProdTable[ProdStatus_$Label] = "ReportedFinished")**:
   - Filters the data to include only products in the "Brand" category group (`'.PRODUCTS'[CATEGORYGROUP] = "Brand"`).
   - Further filters to include only production orders with a status of "Completed" or "ReportedFinished" (`ProdTable[ProdStatus_$Label]`).

The result represents the total quantity of good products produced that are categorized as "Brand" and have a production status of either "Completed" or "ReportedFinished."

measures.name: ACTUALINVOICEQTY
measures.description:
The `ACTUALINVOICEQTY` measure calculates the total invoiced quantity by summing the `CustInvoiceTrans[Qty]` column. It uses an alternative relationship between the delivery date (`CustInvoiceTrans[DlvDate]`) and the date table (`.DATE'[DATES]`) to perform this calculation. 

The result represents the total quantity of items invoiced, adjusted to consider the specified date relationship.

measures.name: PAL_UOM
measures.description: The `PAL_UOM` measure returns the maximum value from the `pal` column in the `.PRODUCTS` table. This value represents the largest pallet unit of measure for the products listed in that table.

measures.name: PLANUNITS
measures.description:
The `PLANUNITS` measure calculates the total number of units from the `PLAN[UNITS]` column, filtered to include only records where the `MAINACCOUNTID` in the `.P&L_ACCOUNTS` table is "40100". Here's how it works:

1. **SUM(PLAN[UNITS])**: Sums up the values in the `PLAN[UNITS]` column, representing the total number of units.

2. **CALCULATE(..., '.P&L_ACCOUNTS'[MAINACCOUNTID] = "40100")**: The `CALCULATE` function applies a filter to include only those records where `MAINACCOUNTID` equals "40100" (typically representing revenue-related data).

This measure provides the total number of units associated with the revenue account defined by `MAINACCOUNTID = "40100"`.

measures.name: FORECASTUNITS
measures.description: The `FORECASTUNITS` measure calculates the total forecasted units by summing the `PLAN[UNITS]` column, filtered specifically for `MAINACCOUNTID` "40100" (typically related to revenue), and for records where `MODELID` is "FORECAST". It removes filters from the `.DEPARTMENT` table and various columns in the `.P&L_ACCOUNTS` table to ensure the calculation considers the entire dataset without these specific filters.

measures.name: BUDGETUNITS
measures.description: The `BUDGETUNITS` measure calculates the total budgeted units by summing the `PLAN[UNITS]` column, filtered specifically for `MAINACCOUNTID` "40100" (typically revenue-related), and for records where `MODELID` is "BUDGET25". It removes any filters from the `.DEPARTMENT` table and multiple columns in the `.P&L_ACCOUNTS` table to ensure the calculation considers the entire dataset without these specific filters.

measures.name: ESTIMATEUNITS
measures.description:
The `ESTIMATEUNITS` measure estimates the total units by combining actual invoiced units up to a specified date with forecasted units beyond that date. Here's how it works:

1. **MaxDate**: Identifies the latest date in the `'.PERIODENDDATES'[PERIODENDDATES]` column.

2. **Actual Invoiced Units**: 
   - **CALCULATE([ACTUALINVOICEDUNITS], KEEPFILTERS('.DATE'[DATES] <= MaxDate), ALL('.DEPARTMENT'[DEPARTMENT]), ALL('.P&L_ACCOUNTS'[ACCOUNT]))**:
   - Calculates the actual invoiced units up to `MaxDate`, removing filters from the `.DEPARTMENT` and `.P&L_ACCOUNTS` tables.

3. **Forecasted Units**:
   - **CALCULATE([FORECASTUNITS], KEEPFILTERS('.DATE'[DATES] > MaxDate), ALL('.CATEGORY'[CATEGORY]), ALL('.P&L_ACCOUNTS'), ALL('.DEPARTMENT'),  '.MODELID'[MODELID] = "FORECAST"))**:
   - Adds forecasted units beyond `MaxDate`, with filters removed from the `.CATEGORY`, `.P&L_ACCOUNTS`, and `.DEPARTMENT` tables, and specifically using the "FORECAST" model.

The measure returns the sum of actual invoiced units up to the period end date and forecasted units thereafter.

measures.name: ACTUALINVOICEDCM
measures.description: The `ACTUALINVOICEDCM` measure calculates the actual invoiced contribution margin by applying the contribution margin percentage (`CM2`) to the actual gross sales value (`[ACTUALGSV]`), filtered up to the latest period end date. It sums revenue, selling costs, and variable costs to determine the contribution margin, then divides by total revenue to get the percentage. The result reflects the contribution margin for invoiced sales up to a specific date.

measures.name: STANDARDCM %
measures.description: The `STANDARDCM %` measure calculates the contribution margin percentage by summing revenue, selling costs, and variable costs, and dividing by the total revenue. It represents the proportion of revenue that covers fixed costs and profit.

measures.name: PLANKG
measures.description:
The `PLANKG` measure calculates the total kilograms (KG) from the `PLAN[KG]` column, but only for records where the `MAINACCOUNTID` in the `.P&L_ACCOUNTS` table is "40100". Here's how it works:

1. **SUM(PLAN[KG])**: Sums up the values in the `PLAN[KG]` column, representing the total weight in kilograms.

2. **CALCULATE(..., '.P&L_ACCOUNTS'[MAINACCOUNTID] = "40100")**: The `CALCULATE` function applies a filter to include only those records where `MAINACCOUNTID` equals "40100" (typically representing revenue-related data).

This measure provides the total weight in kilograms for the revenue account defined by `MAINACCOUNTID = "40100"`.

measures.name: PLANPALLETS
measures.description:
The `PLANPALLETS` measure calculates the total number of pallets from the `PLAN[PALLETS]` column, filtered to include only records where the `MAINACCOUNTID` in the `.P&L_ACCOUNTS` table is "40100". Here's how it works:

1. **SUM(PLAN[PALLETS])**: Sums up the values in the `PLAN[PALLETS]` column, representing the total number of pallets.

2. **CALCULATE(..., '.P&L_ACCOUNTS'[MAINACCOUNTID] = "40100")**: The `CALCULATE` function applies a filter that includes only records where `MAINACCOUNTID` equals "40100" (typically representing revenue-related items).

This measure gives the total number of pallets associated with the revenue account defined by `MAINACCOUNTID = "40100"`.

measures.name: ESTIMATECM
measures.description: The `ESTIMATECM` measure calculates the contribution margin by taking the absolute value of the `ESTIMATE` measure, but only for records where the `COSTGROUP` in `.P&L_ACCOUNTS` is not "FIXED". This excludes fixed costs from the calculation, focusing on variable costs and revenue to determine the contribution margin.

measures.name: ESTIMATEFIXED
measures.description: The `ESTIMATEFIXED` measure calculates the absolute value of the `ESTIMATE` measure specifically for records where the `COSTGROUP` in `.P&L_ACCOUNTS` is "FIXED". This measure focuses on estimating the fixed costs, ensuring the value is always positive.

measures.name: ACTUAL $/KG
measures.description: The `ACTUAL $/KG` measure calculates the estimated value (`[ESTIMATE]`) specifically for activities where the `ACTIVITY` column in the `ACTIVITY` table equals "$/KG". This measure filters the `ESTIMATE` value to reflect only those activities that are measured on a per kilogram basis.

measures.name: FORECAST $/KG
measures.description: The `FORECAST $/KG` measure calculates the forecasted value (`[FORECAST $]`) specifically for activities where the `ACTIVITY` column in the `ACTIVITY` table equals "$/KG". This measure filters the forecasted amount to reflect only those activities that are measured on a per kilogram basis.

measures.name: ACTUALPALLETS PY
measures.description:
The `ACTUALPALLETS PY` measure calculates the actual number of invoiced pallets for the previous fiscal year. Here's how it works:

1. **[ACTUALINVOICEDPALLETS]**: This measure represents the actual number of invoiced pallets in the current context.

2. **CALCULATE(..., '.DATE'[FISCALYEAR] = SELECTEDVALUE('.DATE'[FISCALYEAR], BLANK()) - 1)**: The `CALCULATE` function filters the data to include only records from the previous fiscal year. It does this by selecting the current fiscal year from the `'.DATE'[FISCALYEAR]` column and subtracting 1 to get the previous fiscal year.

The result provides the actual number of invoiced pallets for the previous fiscal year, which can be used for year-over-year analysis.

measures.name: BUDGETNSV
measures.description: The `BUDGETNSV` measure calculates the budgeted net sales value by summing `[BUDGET $]` for records where the `GROUP` in `.P&L_ACCOUNTS` is either "Sales" or "Selling expense". This measure filters the budgeted amount to focus on sales-related and selling expense accounts.

measures.name: ACTUALNSV PY
measures.description:
The `ACTUALNSV PY` measure calculates the actual net sales value (NSV) for the previous fiscal year. It works as follows:

1. **[ACTUALNSV $]**: This is the measure representing the actual net sales value for the current context.

2. **CALCULATE(..., '.DATE'[FISCALYEAR] = SELECTEDVALUE('.DATE'[FISCALYEAR], BLANK()) - 1)**: The `CALCULATE` function filters the data to include only the records from the previous fiscal year. It does this by selecting the current fiscal year from the `'.DATE'[FISCALYEAR]` column, subtracting 1 to get the previous year.

The result of this measure is the net sales value for the previous fiscal year, allowing for year-over-year comparisons.

measures.name: PERIODENDTITLE
measures.description: The `PERIODENDTITLE` measure creates a title string that combines the text "CURRENT PERIOD - " with the selected period end date. It does this by using the `CONCATENATE` function to join the text with the value returned by `SELECTEDVALUE('.PERIODENDDATES'[PERIODEND])`, which represents the period end date selected in the context. The resulting title dynamically reflects the current period being analyzed.

measures.name: ACTUALINVOICEDKG
measures.description:
The `ACTUALINVOICEDKG` measure calculates the total invoiced kilograms by multiplying the actual invoiced quantity (`[ACTUALINVOICEQTY]`) by the kilogram unit of measure (`[KG_UOM]`) for each product. It then sums these values, considering only invoices up to the latest period end date (`MaxDate`). The measure also uses an alternative relationship between the delivery date (`CustInvoiceTrans[DlvDate]`) and the date table (`.DATE'[DATES]`). 

The result represents the total kilograms invoiced up to the specified period end date.

measures.name: PLANASOFDATE
measures.description:
The `PLANASOFDATE` measure calculates the most recent date from the `PLAN[PLANASATDATE]` column and formats this date and time as "dd/MM/yyyy hh:mm:ss AM/PM". Here's how it works:

1. **MAX(PLAN[PLANASATDATE])**: Retrieves the maximum (most recent) date from the `PLAN[PLANASATDATE]` column.

2. **VAR AdjustedDate**: Stores this maximum date in a variable called `AdjustedDate`.

3. **FORMAT(AdjustedDate, "dd/MM/yyyy hh:mm:ss AM/PM")**: Formats the `AdjustedDate` variable to display the date and time in the "dd/MM/yyyy hh:mm:ss AM/PM" format.

The result is the most recent "as of" date and time for the plan, presented in a standard date-time format.

measures.name: ACTUALPRODUCTIONCOST/KG
measures.description:
The ACTUALPRODUCTIONCOST/KG measure calculates the actual production cost per kilogram. Here's how it works:

SUM(ProdCalcTrans[RealCostAmount]): This part sums up the RealCostAmount from the ProdCalcTrans table, representing the total actual cost incurred during production.

[ACTUALPRODUCTIONKG]: This is a measure or column that represents the total weight of the production in kilograms.

DIVIDE(... , ..., BLANK()): The DIVIDE function divides the total actual cost by the total production weight in kilograms. If the total production weight ([ACTUALPRODUCTIONKG]) is zero, the measure returns a blank value to avoid division by zero.
